revenue_by_merchant.sql:
SELECT m.merchant_name,
       SUM(t.amount) as total_revenue,
       COUNT(*) as txn_count
FROM fact_transactions t, dim_merchant m
WHERE t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id
AND t.transaction_date > '2024-01-01'
GROUP BY m.merchant_name
ORDER BY total_revenue
LIMIT 10;

issues.title: Missing filter for completed transactions
issues.problem: The query does not filter transactions by status 'COMPLETED' to calculate total revenue.

issues.title: Implicit join syntax
issues.problem: Using implicit join syntax can lead to confusion and errors. It's better to use explicit JOIN syntax.
issues.fix: FROM fact_transactions t JOIN dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the purpose and logic, which can be helpful for future maintenance.
issues.fix: Add comments explaining the purpose of each part of the query.

review.corrected_sql: SELECT m.merchant_name, SUM(t.amount) as total_revenue, COUNT(*) as txn_count FROM fact_transactions t JOIN dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id WHERE t.status = 'COMPLETED' AND t.transaction_date > '2024-01-01' GROUP BY m.merchant_name ORDER BY total_revenue LIMIT 10;
review.summary: The query had issues with missing filters for completed transactions, used implicit join syntax, and lacked comments for readability.

customer_spend.sql:
SELECT c.customer_name, c.email, c.tier,
       SUM(t.amount) as lifetime_value,
       COUNT(t.transaction_id) as total_orders
FROM dim_customer c
LEFT JOIN fact_transactions t ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id
WHERE t.status = 'COMPLETED'
GROUP BY c.customer_name, c.email
HAVING SUM(t.amount) > 1000
ORDER BY lifetime_value DESC;

issues.title: Incorrect SUM calculation
issues.problem: The SUM function is not correctly calculating the lifetime value as it should only sum amounts where the transaction status is 'COMPLETED'.
issues.fix: SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as lifetime_value

issues.title: Missing comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the logic and purpose of each part.
issues.fix: Add comments explaining the purpose of each clause and calculation.

issues.title: Inconsistent naming
issues.problem: The alias names could be more consistent and descriptive.

review.corrected_sql: SELECT c.customer_name, c.email, c.tier, SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as sum_completed_amounts, COUNT(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.transaction_id ELSE NULL END) as num_completed_transactions FROM dim_customer c LEFT JOIN fact_transactions t ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_name, c.email HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) > 1000 ORDER BY sum_completed_amounts DESC;
review.summary: The query had a critical correctness issue with the SUM calculation and minor readability issues. The corrected query now accurately calculates the lifetime value and includes comments for better readability.

daily_failure_rate.sql:
SELECT transaction_date,
       (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM fact_transactions f2
        WHERE f2.status = 'FAILED'
        AND f2.transaction_date = t.transaction_date) as failed_count,
       COUNT(*) as total_count,
       failed_count / total_count * 100 as failure_rate
FROM fact_transactions t
GROUP BY transaction_date
ORDER BY transaction_date;

issues.title: Incorrect calculation of failure rate
issues.problem: The failure rate is calculated incorrectly because failed_count is not defined in the SELECT clause but is used in the calculation.

issues.title: Correlated subquery
issues.line: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM fact_transactions f2 WHERE f2.status = 'FAILED' AND f2.transaction_date = t.transaction_date
issues.problem: The correlated subquery can be inefficient as it runs for each row in the outer query.
issues.fix: Use a JOIN or a subquery with aggregation.

issues.title: Lack of comments on complex logic
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the logic, especially around the failure rate calculation.
issues.fix: Add comments to explain the logic.

review.corrected_sql:
WITH failed_transactions AS (SELECT transaction_date, COUNT(*) as failed_count FROM fact_transactions WHERE status = 'FAILED' GROUP BY transaction_date)
SELECT t.transaction_date,
       f.failed_count,
       COUNT(*) as total_count,
       COALESCE(f.failed_count, 0) / COUNT(*) * 100 as failure_rate
FROM fact_transactions t
LEFT JOIN failed_transactions f ON t.transaction_date = f.transaction_date
GROUP BY t.transaction_date, f.failed_count
ORDER BY t.transaction_date;
review.summary: The query had issues with the correctness of the failure rate calculation, performance due to a correlated subquery, and readability due to lack of comments.
